sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.108 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: For a finite group$G$let$(G)$denote the set of irreducible character codegrees of$G$(see 20.78). Define$(G)= \{ | (m)|: \, m (G)\}$, where$(m)$denotes the set of prime divisors of an integer$m$. It is proved that there exists a~constant$k$such that$| (G)| k (G)$for every finite group$G$(Y.\,Yang, G.\,Qian, J. Algebra, { 478} (2017), 215--219), but the estimate provided for$k$is very crude.
